evidence: intro_hook 4/10 — The post opens with repeated navigation chrome ('Blog Glossary… Glossary…') followed by a boilerplate TL;DR label. There is no earned hook—no surprising stat, no tension, no specific failure scenario. The reader has no reason to keep reading. The first two actual sentences ('AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to…' and 'In 2026, AEO, GEO, and LLMO should be treated…') are definitional throat-clearing. The 11%-citation-gap stat buried mid-post is the real hook and should open the piece.

suggestedPatch.before: Blog Glossary What Is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? Glossary What Is Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)? The Complete 2026 Definition TL;DR: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the practice of optimizing your content to be cited and recommended by AI-powered answer engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Only 11% of B2B SaaS brands appear when a buyer asks ChatGPT a category question at 11pm—without naming the brand. Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the discipline of closing that gap: getting your brand cited directly inside AI-generated responses, not just ranked in blue links.
